perturbation.original_text: Receiver shall use the same degree of care to protect and safeguard the confidentiality of Confidential Information as it uses to protect and safeguard the confidentiality of its own proprietary info rmation, and Receiver represents, warrants and covenants to VIDAR that such degree of care is reasonably designed to protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiver shall use a reasonable degree of care, but no less than ordinary care, to protect and safeguard the confidentiality of Confidential Information. Receiver makes no representation or warranty regarding the effectiveness of its measures to protect Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by weakening Receiver's obligation to protect Confidential Information. The original text requires the same degree of care as used for its own proprietary information, coupled with a warranty. The changed text removes the 'same degree of care' standard and eliminates the warranty. This change contradicts the Injunction clause (Section 8), which assumes that any disclosure or unauthorized use of Confidential Information would cause irreparable harm and loss to VIDAR. The weakened protection standard increases the risk of disclosure, undermining the basis for injunctive relief.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiver acknowledges and agrees that: (i) the Confidential Information is proprietary to and valuable information of VIDAR; (ii) VIDAR derives economic value from the Confidential Information not being generally known to other persons who can obtain economic value from its discl osure or use; (iii) any disclosure or unauthorized use of the Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm and loss to VIDAR; and (iv) if this Agr eement is breached, VIDAR can not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, VIDAR, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent further breaches of this Agreem ent and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. Receiver shall reimburse VIDAR for all costs and expenses, including attorney’s fees, incurr ed by VIDAR in enforcing the obligations of Receiver.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the receiver's obligation to protect confidential information by replacing the 'same degree of care' standard with a 'reasonable degree of care, but no less than ordinary care' standard and removing the warranty regarding the effectiveness of the measures. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that any disclosure or unauthorized use of confidential information could cause irreparable harm to VIDAR and that VIDAR is entitled to an injunction to prevent further breaches. The weakened protection standard increases the risk of disclosure, undermining the basis for injunctive relief, therefore, it is a strong contradiction based on the explanation given and based on definition 5 (Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction).

perturbation.original_text: Receiver may di sclose Confidential Information if the same: (a) was generally available at the time it was disclosed other than as a result of a disclosure made by Receiver; (b) was known to the Receiver at the time it received such information, as evidenced by contemporaneous documentati on in Receiver’s files; (c) is disclosed with the prior written approval of VIDAR; (d) becomes known to Receiver on a non-conf idential basis from a source other than VIDAR without breach of this Agreement by Receiver; (e) is disclosed to a third party by VIDA R without restrictions against non-disclosure similar to those contained in this Agreement; (f) is disclosed pursuant to the order or requirement of a court; administrative agency, or other government body, provi ded Receiver takes all reasonable steps to keep the Confidential Information from becoming part of public records; or (g) is hereafter independently developed by Rece iver without the aid, application or use of any Confidential Information; provided for each that: (i) Receiver promptly notifies VIDAR of such action; and (ii) in any action to enforce or remedy a breach of this Agreement, Receiver shall have the burden of establishing the applicability of the subsection on which it relies.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiver may disclose Confidential Information if the same: (a) was generally available at the time it was disclosed other than as a result of a disclosure made by Receiver; (b) was known to the Receiver at the time it received such information, as evidenced by contemporaneous documentati on in Receiver’s files; (c) is disclosed with the prior written approval of VIDAR; (d) becomes known to Receiver on a non-conf idential basis from a source other than VIDAR without breach of this Agreement by Receiver; (e) is disclosed to a third party by VIDA R without restrictions against non-disclosure similar to those contained in this Agreement; (f) is disclosed pursuant to the order or requirement of a court; administrative agency, or other government body; or (g) is hereafter independently developed by Rece iver without the aid, application or use of any Confidential Information; provided for each that: (i) Receiver promptly notifies VIDAR of such action. **However, Receiver is not required to notify VIDAR if the disclosure is legally mandated under subsection (f)**; and (ii) in any action to enforce or remedy a breach of this Agreement, Receiver shall have the burden of establishing the applicability of the subsection on which it relies.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 3 by adding an exception to the notification requirement. While the original clause (i) states that the Receiver must promptly notify VIDAR of any disclosure, the modified clause now exempts legally mandated disclosures under subsection (f) from this notification requirement. This conflicts with the purpose of the notification clause, which is to allow VIDAR to take steps to protect its Confidential Information. By removing the notification requirement for legally mandated disclosures, VIDAR may be unaware of such disclosures and unable to take appropriate action, such as seeking a protective order.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Additional Disclosures. Receiver may di sclose Confidential Information if the same: (a) was generally available at the time it was disclosed other than as a result of a disclosure made by Receiver; (b) was known to the Receiver at the time it received such information, as evidenced by contemporaneous documentati on in Receiver’s files; (c) is disclosed with the prior written approval of VIDAR; (d) becomes known to Receiver on a non-conf idential basis from a source other than VIDAR without breach of this Agreement by Receiver; (e) is disclosed to a third party by VIDA R without restrictions against non-disclosure similar to those contained in this Agreement; (f) is disclosed pursuant to the order or requirement of a court; administrative agency, or other government body; or (g) is hereafter independently developed by Rece iver without the aid, application or use of any Confidential Information; provided for each that: (i) Receiver promptly notifies VIDAR of such action. However, Receiver is not required to notify VIDAR if the disclosure is legally mandated under subsection (f); and (ii) in any action to enforce or remedy a breach of this Agreement, Receiver shall have the burden of establishing the applicability of the subsection on which it relies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original notification requirement for disclosures of confidential information. The original text states that Receiver must notify VIDAR of any disclosure. The changed text adds an exception, stating that Receiver is not required to notify VIDAR if the disclosure is legally mandated under subsection (f). This directly contradicts the original notification requirement, thus, it is a strong in-text contradiction as it creates uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: An employee, director, officer, manager, member, partner, affiliate, associate, agent, attorney, accountant, consultant, banker, business adviser, financial adviser, scientific adviser or technical adviser of Receiver may become a party to this Agreement by si gning a counterpart hereof, a copy of which shall be provided to VIDAR wit hin five days of signature.
perturbation.changed_text: An employee of Receiver may become a party to this Agreement by signing a counterpart hereof, a copy of which shall be provided to VIDAR within five days of signature. **However, no other parties, including directors, officers, managers, members, partners, affiliates, associates, agents, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers, business advisers, financial advisers, scientific advisers or technical advisers of Receiver can become a party to this agreement.**
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates a contradiction in Section 4. It initially states that specific individuals associated with the Receiver may become a party to the agreement by signing a counterpart. However, the addition explicitly prohibits all other parties listed from becoming part of this agreement. This is an in-text contradiction that creates ambiguity regarding who is authorized to become a party to this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Additional Receivers. An employee of Receiver may become a party to this Agreement by signing a counterpart hereof, a copy of which shall be provided to VIDAR within five days of signature. However, no other parties, including directors, officers, managers, members, partners, affiliates, associates, agents, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers, business advisers, financial advisers, scientific advisers or technical advisers of Receiver can become a party to this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly prohibits parties (directors, officers, etc.) from becoming a party to the agreement. The original text allows for those parties to become a part of the agreement under certain conditions. Because the Changed Text explicitly states that no other parties other than an employee can be a party to the agreement, that directly contradicts the original text. The contradicted text and the changed text are also the same, meaning the contradiction is an in-text contradiction. Therefore, the modification introduces a strong, direct contradiction, making the answer YES.

perturbation.original_text: VIDAR may terminate this Agreement at any time for any reason.
perturbation.changed_text: VIDAR may terminate this Agreement at any time for any reason. **However, VIDAR may only terminate this Agreement if Receiver has breached the terms set forth herein.**
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction to the contract. The original clause gives VIDAR the right to terminate the agreement at any time for any reason. The added text restricts this right, specifying that VIDAR may only terminate if the Receiver has breached the terms of the agreement. This creates uncertainty about the circumstances under which the agreement can be terminated and weakens the original clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General. This Agreement shall be gov erned by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia and all actions involving this Agreement shall be brought in the state or federal courts located in the Commonwealth of Virginia, to which the parties consent to jurisdiction. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties, their legal representatives, successors and assigns. Except as otherwise expressly provided herein, neither this Agreement, nor any rights granted hereunder may be assigned, transferred, conveyed or encumbered, whether voluntarily or by operation of law, by Receiver without the prior written consent of VI DAR (which may be granted or withheld in VIDAR’s sole and absolute judgment), and any attempt to do so will be deemed null and void. This Agreement becomes effectiv e on the Effective Date and continues in effect thereafter for so long as any Conf idential Information continues to exist. VIDAR may terminate this Agreement at any time for any reason. However, VIDAR may only terminate this Agreement if Receiver has breached the terms set forth herein. Any provision of this Agreement which is prohibited, unenforceable or not authorized in any jurisdiction shall, as to such jurisdicti on, be ineffective to the extent of such prohibition, unenforceability or non-authorization without in validating the remaining
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states VIDAR may terminate for "any reason". The changed text adds "However, VIDAR may only terminate this Agreement if Receiver has breached the terms set forth herein." This new condition directly contradicts the original broad termination right, creating a strong in-text inconsistency because the same sentence now provides conflicting reasons that VIDAR can terminate the agreement. Based on the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition provided, this 'creates in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.'

perturbation.original_text: Any provision of this Agreement which is prohibited, unenforceable or not authorized in any jurisdiction shall, as to such jurisdicti on, be ineffective to the extent of such prohibition, unenforceability or non-authorization without in validating the remaining
perturbation.changed_text: Any provision of this Agreement which is prohibited, unenforceable or not authorized in any jurisdiction shall, as to such jurisdicti on, be ineffective to the extent of such prohibition, unenforceability or non-authorization **and shall immediately render the entire agreement void and unenforceable, with no recourse from either party.** without in validating the remaining
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the 'severability' or 'savings' clause, which attempts to preserve the agreement even if parts are invalid. By stating that any unenforceable provision renders the *entire* agreement void, it negates the purpose of the original severability language. This creates uncertainty in enforcement, as the severability clause is meant to ensure that the rest of the agreement remains valid even if one part fails.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General. This Agreement shall be gov erned by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia and all actions involving this Agreement shall be brought in the state or federal courts located in the Commonwealth of Virginia, to which the parties consent to jurisdiction. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties, their legal representatives, successors and assigns. Except as otherwise expressly provided herein, neither this Agreement, nor any rights granted hereunder may be assigned, transferred, conveyed or encumbered, whether voluntarily or by operation of law, by Receiver without the prior written consent of VI DAR (which may be granted or withheld in VIDAR’s sole and absolute judgment), and any attempt to do so will be deemed null and void. This Agreement becomes effectiv e on the Effective Date and continues in effect thereafter for so long as any Conf idential Information continues to exist. VIDAR may terminate this Agreement at any time for any reason. However, VIDAR may only terminate this Agreement if Receiver has breached the terms set forth herein. Any provision of this Agreement which is prohibited, unenforceable or not authorized in any jurisdiction shall, as to such jurisdicti on, be ineffective to the extent of such prohibition, unenforceability or non-authorization and shall immediately render the entire agreement void and unenforceable, with no recourse from either party. without in validating the remaining
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "and shall immediately render the entire agreement void and unenforceable, with no recourse from either party." to the original text. This directly contradicts the portion of the contradicted text that states "Any provision of this Agreement which is prohibited, unenforceable or not authorized in any jurisdiction shall, as to such jurisdicti on, be ineffective to the extent of such prohibition, unenforceability or non-authorization without in validating the remaining." The changed text specifies the entire agreement is void, whereas the original text (and contradicted text) attempts to preserve the agreement through severability.
